# /frontier surfaces — the per-surface trust map (DATA, regenerated by /frontier refresh)

The DATA half of /frontier (method lives in SKILL.md). This is NOT a fixed tool list — each surface declares: what it covers, its AXES, the BENCHMARK exemplars to measure against, the SOURCES to ask "what is frontier now," the artifacts it reads, and the executor to delegate adoption to. Sources stay stable for years even as the tools inside them churn — that is what survives the freshness problem.

This is an EXAMPLE with one surface (design) filled in. Add your own — model-stack, infra, content, agents — by appending a block, or via `/frontier refresh`.

## surface: design   [SEED — re-verify with /frontier refresh]
Covers: the visual layer — your DESIGN_SYSTEM.md, rendered cards, the repo's UI / site pages.
Axes: product-craft AND emotional/motion/warmth (human-centered = warmth + emotion + a11y, not minimal-only).
Benchmark exemplars (re-FETCH live each run): Apple HIG (developer.apple.com/design), Linear (linear.app), Figma (figma.com) [product-craft]; Pixar (story-first / emotional craft, make-them-feel), poolday.ai (AI-video; "AI amplifies human artistry, the creator stays in command" — agentic-creative warmth + cinematic motion) [emotional axis].
Frontier-tool sources (query live, gate before proposing): shadcn registry ecosystem (ui.shadcn.com, 21st.dev, MagicUI, Aceternity); Tailwind + Radix; tokens (Style Dictionary, Tokens Studio); motion (Motion, GSAP, View Transitions API); icons/fonts (lucide, variable fonts via fontsource); render/a11y (headless Chrome, Playwright, axe-core); the Figma MCP bridge.
Reads: your DESIGN_SYSTEM.md, your rendered cards, the repo's frontend.
Executor: a shadcn-CLI driver (+ a card renderer for the visual check).

## Add your own surfaces (sketches to define for your own work)
- model-stack: your LLM/AI stack — model choice, API patterns (structured outputs, caching, MCP), provider routing. Reads your provider-routing config + requirements.txt + the claude-api reference.
- agents: agent architecture — orchestration (multi-agent / workflows), eval + grading, memory, tool design.
- infra: deploy / runtime — your cloud provider, Actions/CI, scheduling, observability.
- content: public surface — landing/pitch pages, build-in-public craft.

## Anti-slop gate (a candidate must clear ALL before it can enter an adoption plan)
maintained (release <6mo, alive) · adopted (real downloads/stars, or standardized-on) · known provenance (named org or a reviewed registry) · fits the stack · prefer source-you-own over heavyweight runtime deps.

Last full live-verify: NONE (seed). The newest dated entry governs the 30-day staleness self-check.
